title: High Rise Fire Sprinkler Incentive Act of 2025
short_title: High Rise Fire Sprinkler Incentive Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill helps owners of tall apartment buildings save money on taxes when they add fire sprinkler systems. Instead of waiting many years to get tax breaks for this safety equipment, building owners can spread the costs over just fifteen years. This faster tax schedule makes it cheaper for them to install sprinklers that can save lives in fires. The goal is to encourage more building owners to add these important safety systems to older tall buildings that might not have them yet.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Ways and Means.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the High Rise Fire Sprinkler Incentive Act of twenty twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Classification of Certain Automatic Fire Sprinkler System Retrofits
sections.text: This section changes the tax rules for certain fire sprinkler systems. When a building owner installs a fire sprinkler system in an existing high rise building, they can now treat it as fifteen year property for tax purposes. This means they can deduct the cost of the system over fifteen years instead of a longer period. The system must meet safety standards set by the National Fire Protection Association. It must be installed in a residential building that was built before the sprinkler system was added. The building must also be tall enough that it has a floor more than seventy five feet above where fire trucks can park to reach the building. These tax changes take effect after this law is signed.
